# PostHTML

Give AI agents the ability to upload, edit, and share HTML plans programmatically via CLI.

## Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router, Drizzle ORM, Neon Postgres
- Better Auth (Google OAuth only) + `@better-auth/api-key` plugin
- CLI: post/posthtml (npm package)

## Key files
- `web/lib/auth.ts` — Better Auth config
- `web/lib/auth-client.ts` — auth client
- `web/db/schema.ts` — Drizzle schema (6 tables)
- `web/app/page.tsx` — landing page + Google sign-in
- `web/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — API key management UI
- `cli/src/index.ts` — CLI entrypoint (commander)
- `cli/src/config.ts` — config file loader/saver

## Auth flow
1. User signs in with Google via landing page
2. Session cookie for dashboard
3. Dashboard generates API keys
4. CLI uses `x-api-key` header → `auth.api.verifyApiKey()` on plan routes

## API auth
- Dashboard routes: session cookie
- Plan/CLI routes: `x-api-key` header (verified via Better Auth `verifyApiKey`)
- Public viewer `/p/:id`: no auth

## Installation

### CLI
```bash
npm install -g @androff/posthtml-cli
```

Configure your API key:
```bash
post setup --key <your-key>
# or set env vars:
export POST_API_KEY=***
export POST_URL=https://posthtml.vercel.app
```

### Commands
```bash
post upload index.html                              # upload a plan
post upload index.html --data '{"status":"draft"}'   # upload + attach JSON data in one call
post upload index.html --data-file meta.json         # or merge a whole JSON file
post upload index.html --private                     # create as private (owner-only)
post upload index.html --public                      # create as public (default)
post replace <id> file.html --private                # update + set private (preserves URL)
post replace <id> file.html --public                 # update + set public
post list                                            # list your plans
post delete <id>                                     # delete a plan
post replace <id> file.html                          # update plan content (preserves URL, html only)
post data get <id>                                   # read a plan's JSON data
post data set <id> --key <k> --value '<json>'        # merge one key into a plan's JSON data
post data set <id> --file data.json                  # merge a whole JSON file into a plan's data
```

## Plan data (`data` field)

Every plan has an arbitrary JSON `data` object alongside its `html`, for anything that
isn't markup — status, task metadata, structured context, whatever the agent needs to
track about the plan over time. It's not auto-populated: use `--data`/`--data-file` on
`upload`, or `post data set` at any point after. Reading it back (`post data get`
or `GET /api/plans/:id/data`) requires no auth for public plans — same as the plan viewer.
Private plans (`is_private=true`) require the owner's session cookie or `x-api-key`: anonymous
requests get 401, other users get 403. Mark a plan private with `post upload --private` /
`post replace --private`, or flip it anytime via `PATCH /api/plans/:id` with `{ "isPrivate": true }`.

`post replace` only updates `html` — it has no `--data` flag. If a plan needs new html
*and* new data together, create a fresh plan with `post upload --data` and delete the old
one if it's no longer needed, rather than replacing in place.

## Quick test
```bash
POST_URL=https://posthtml.vercel.app POST_API_KEY=*** post list
```
